warnings: Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: Aqueous acid dissociation constant pKa (HA ⇌ A⁻ + H⁺)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding
Geometry provenance: user-supplied protonated (HA) and deprotonated (A⁻) methylammonium XYZ files, each re-optimized (opt-freq) at the same GFN2-xTB level in an implicit solvent field (no imaginary frequencies found; species judged minima)
Solvent / environment: ALPB implicit water continuum (no explicit solvent)
Charges / multiplicities: HA +1, singlet (1); A⁻ 0, singlet (1); H⁺ reference −270.28 kcal mol⁻¹ (Tissandier 1998 standard)
Software: xtb via chemkit pka-acidity skill (chemkit engine calling xtb; program tag "xtb")
Convergence & caveats: All species converged; 0 genuine imaginary modes. Density fitting inherent to xTB. Warnings reproduced below. Single-conformer protocol; absolute pKa in xTB is suitable only for qualitative screening.

Headline result
Computed absolute aqueous pKa (GFN2-xTB/ALPB, 298 K, 1 atm, single-conformer):
pKa = −117.62959350681938

Live log: see /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/pka-acidity-validation/methylammonium/20260626-151446_methylammonium_pka_acidity/pka_20260626-151507.out (written during run; contains full SCF and optimizer details)

Warnings (verbatim):
• Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.
• Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

Note: GFN2-xTB absolute pKa values are not quantitatively reliable; they are best used for relative trend screening rather than definitive prediction.
